steps.title: Extract the roots from factored form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = (x+3)(x+1)$ is already in factored form. Setting each factor to zero gives us the roots: when $x + 3 = 0$, we get $x = -3$; when $x + 1 = 0$, we get $x = -1$.

steps.title: Find the vertex x-coordinate
steps.content:
For a parabola in factored form, the vertex lies at the axis of symmetry, which passes through the midpoint of the two roots. Substituting our roots into the vertex formula:
$$\frac{-3 + (-1)}{2} = \frac{-4}{2} = -2$$
Now check which interval contains $-2$:
  • A) $-4 < x < -3$ — does not include $-2$
  • B) $-3 < x < 1$ — includes $-2$ since $-3 < -2 < 1$
  • C) $-2 < x < 3$ — does not include $-2$
  • D) $-2 < x < 4$ — does not include $-2$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
